zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond於大約1560年出生在英國薩默塞特韋斯特 邦藍時，她的父親Sir George Bond27歲，母親Winifred Leigh24歲。她於1574年與George Strong在英國英格蘭薩默塞特哈德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子的父母。她去世於大約1628年在英國戴文哈德斯托可享年70歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦比曉普斯蓋特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond于大约1560年出生在英国薩默塞特韋斯特 邦藍时，她的父亲Sir George Bond27岁，母亲Winifred Leigh24岁。她于1574年与George Strong在英国英格兰薩默塞特哈德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子的父母。她去世于大约1628年在英国戴文哈德斯托可享年70岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦比曉普斯蓋特。

nameMeanings.name: Bond
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name for a peasant farmer or husbandman, Middle English bond(e), bounde, occasionally bande ‘bondman, customary tenant, serf’ (Old English bonda, bunda, reinforced by Old Norse bóndi). The Old Norse word was also in use as a personal name (Old Norse Bóndi, Bondi, Bundi, Bonde, borrowed as late Old English Bonda), and this has given rise to other English and Scandinavian surnames alongside those originating as status names, such as the Middle English personal name Bonde. The status of the peasant farmer fluctuated considerably during the Middle Ages; moreover, the underlying ancient Germanic word is of disputed origin and meaning. Among ancient Germanic peoples who settled to an agricultural life, the term came to signify a farmer holding lands from, and bound by loyalty to, a lord; from this developed the sense of a free landholder as opposed to a serf. In England after the Norman Conquest the word sank in status and became associated with the notion of bound servitude. The name can also be a variant of Band .
Swedish: variant of   Bonde  .
In some cases also an American shortened form of Ukrainian Bondarenko and possibly also of some other surname beginning with Bond-.

variants.name: Bundock
meanings: English (Essex): from a Middle English personal name Bondoc, a pet form of Bonde (late Old English Bonda; see Bond 1) + the Old English hypocoristic suffix -oc. Compare Bundick .

variants.name: Husband
meanings: English and Scottish (Fife): status name from Middle English housbond(e) ‘male spouse’, ‘householder, male head of a household’, ‘villein or customary tenant who holds land additional to the croft attached to his homestead’, ‘husbandman or farmer’. The term (late Old English hūsbonda, Old Norse húsbóndi) is a compound of hús ‘house’ + bóndi (see Bond ).

variants.name: Bonde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Anders, Erik.
English (Devon): variant of   Bond  .
Danish and Swedish: status name for a farmer, from Old Norse bóndi ‘farmer’ (see also Bond ).

variants.name: Bunde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Gerhard, Hans, Kurt.
North German: status name for a farmer from Middle Low German bunde, bunne, related to Danish, Swedish bonde ‘free peasant’ (see Bond ).

German (Bünde): topographic name from Old High German biunda ‘enclosed fertile pasture’ (modern German Bünde, Beunte), a derivative of biwenden ‘to bend or weave’, with reference to the practice of making fences from woven branches. There are many different local forms and communal implications concerning the use of the land.

variants.name: Bondarenko
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Russian Oleg, Vladimir, Yuriy, Aleksandr, Anatoliy, Anatoly, Gennady, Grigoriy, Igor, Konstantin, Lyubov, Lyudmila.
Ukrainian: patronymic from bondar ‘cooper’, formed with the diminutive suffix -enko. Compare Bond .

variants.name: Bonds
meanings: English: variant of Bond , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Bounds
meanings: English: variant of Bond , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Bound
meanings: English (southern): variant of   Bond  .

variants.name: Bundy
meanings: variant of Bond , either from Middle English bonde, bounde ‘customary tenant’ where the final -e has developed in local speech to -y, or occasionally from the Old Norse Bondi, where final -i has survived.
alternatively, a variant of   Bandy  .
English:

variants.name: Band
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Isidor, Myer.

English, German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a maker of hoops and bands, etc., from Middle English band, bond, Middle High German, Middle Low German bant, German Band ‘hoop, metal band, fetter, shackle’, i.e. something used for tying or binding.
English: variant of   Bond  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond was born about 1560, in West Buckland, Somerset, England, her father, Sir George Bond, was 27 and her mother, Winifred Leigh, was 24. She married George Strong in 1574, in Chard, Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons. She died about 1628, in Chardstock, Devon, England, at the age of 70, and was buried in Bishopsgate, London, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé George Strong en 1574, à Chard, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils. Elle est décédée environ 1628, à Chardstock, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Bishopsgate, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond(이)가 대략 1560년 영국 서머세트 웨스트 벅랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir George Bond(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Winifred Leigh(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1574년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 처드에서 George Strong(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1628년 영국 데폰 처스톡에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 비숍스가트에 묻혔다.

ru.Walbrook St Stephen's Church, London, En: валбрук ст стифен'с чурч, Лондон, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Mary Ann Bond родилась около 1560 г. в местности вест буккланд, сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir George Bond было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Winifred Leigh, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени George Strong в 1574 г. в местности чард, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей. Она умерла около 1628 г. в местности чардстокк, девон, Англия в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности бишопсгэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.
